name: interactive-planner
description: Use Claude Code's interactive question tool to gather comprehensive requirements through structured multi-select questions
capabilities: Ask 4 structured questions per batch with 2-4 options each
Design question categories covering functionality, architecture, UX, quality, and constraints
Synthesize answers into actionable specification documents
Support 20-30 questions across 5-8 batches for comprehensive coverage
author: DNYoussef
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing no executable code. The static scanner produced false positives by misinterpreting documentation syntax and diagram labels. SKILL.md contains only Markdown documentation and YAML examples. interactive-planner-process.dot is a GraphViz flowchart definition. skill-report.json is standard metadata. All findings evaluated are false positives.
valueStatement: Transform vague project ideas into clear specifications through structured multi-choice questions. Eliminate ambiguity and missed requirements with systematic interactive planning sessions.
limitations: Maximum 4 questions per AskUserQuestion tool call
Requires user interaction - cannot work autonomously
Limited to predefined option sets, may miss edge cases
Best for new projects, less useful for simple changes

useCases.title: Define MVP Features
useCases.description: Scope minimum viable products by asking stakeholders about must-have features, technical constraints, and quality requirements.

useCases.title: Client Requirements Gathering
useCases.description: Collect project requirements from clients using structured questions about technology, timeline, budget, and priorities.

useCases.title: Technical Architecture Planning
useCases.description: Make informed decisions about tech stack, deployment, authentication, and scalability through interactive planning.

promptTemplates.title: New Project Planning
promptTemplates.prompt: Use interactive-planner to gather comprehensive requirements for a new e-learning platform with video streaming, user progress tracking, and payment integration. Ask 20-30 questions across multiple batches.

promptTemplates.title: Feature Addition
promptTemplates.scenario: Adding real-time chat to existing app
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to add real-time messaging to my React app. Use interactive questions to understand requirements for chat features, message storage, notifications, and scalability needs.

promptTemplates.title: Refactoring Decision
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me plan the refactoring of a monolithic PHP application. Use structured questions to determine migration approach, new architecture, testing strategy, and deployment plan.

promptTemplates.title: Integration Planning
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to integrate Stripe payments and SendGrid emails. Use interactive planning to gather requirements for payment flows, email templates, error handling, and webhook implementation.

bestPractices: Start with broad questions about project type and goals, then progress to specific technical details
Use multi-select for features and integrations where users might want multiple options
Ask 20-30 questions total across 5-8 batches for comprehensive coverage
antiPatterns: Don't use for simple one-file changes - ask directly instead
Avoid overlapping question categories that collect duplicate information
Don't proceed with insufficient answers - ask clarifying follow-up questions

faq.question: How many questions should I ask?
faq.answer: Ask 20-30 questions across 5-8 batches for comprehensive coverage. Simple projects need fewer.

faq.question: Can users select multiple options?
faq.answer: Yes, enable multiSelect for questions where choices are not mutually exclusive.

faq.question: What if users keep choosing Other?
faq.answer: Your questions are too restrictive. Switch to open discussion or broaden options.

faq.question: How do I handle vague answers?
faq.answer: Ask follow-up questions for clarification before proceeding to next batch.

faq.question: Can I use this for existing projects?
faq.answer: Best for new projects. For existing work, ask specific questions about changes needed.

faq.question: What happens after gathering requirements?
faq.answer: The skill synthesizes answers into a specification document for implementation planning.
